analysis.semantic_summary: Parser processes Axway SFTP server logs capturing file transfer activities including uploads, downloads, deletions, renames, and login events. It extracts session details, user actions, file metadata, and operation results for security monitoring and audit purposes.

factors: Complex regex pattern for log matching

optimization_opportunities: Consolidate multiple activity_id mappings into a lookup table
Simplify regex pattern by breaking into smaller components
Implement caching for repeated constant mappings
Add error handling for malformed log entries
analysis.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: axway_sftp-latest 
-- OCSF Class: File System Activity (1006)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T12:14:40.288793

-- Pre-compile patterns for better performance
local PATTERNS = {
    timestamp = "^(%d%d%d%d%-%d%d%-%d%dT%d%d:%d%d:%d%dZ)",
    log = "AxwaySFTP%s+session_id=\"([^\"]+)\"%s+user=\"([^\"]+)\"%s+event=\"([^\"]+)\".-filePath=\"([^\"]*)\".-%s+result=\"([^\"]+)\"%s+message=\"([^\"]+)\""
}

-- Activity ID lookup table for O(1) mapping
local ACTIVITY_MAPPINGS = {
    LOGIN = {id = 1, name = "Login"},
    UPLOAD = {id = 2, name = "Create"},
    DOWNLOAD = {id = 3, name = "Read"},
    DELETE = {id = 4, name = "Delete"},
    RENAME = {id = 5, name = "Rename"}
}

-- Severity mappings
local SEVERITY_MAPPINGS = {
    SUCCESS = {id = 1, name = "Informational"},
    FAILURE = {id = 3, name = "High"}
}

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" or not record.raw then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize local variables for performance
    local raw = record.raw
    local session_id, username, event_type, file_path, result, message = 
        raw:match(PATTERNS.log)

    -- Validate required fields
    if not session_id or not username or not event_type then
        return nil, "Missing required fields"
    end

    -- Get activity mapping
    local activity = ACTIVITY_MAPPINGS[event_type] or {id = 99, name = "Other"}
    local severity = SEVERITY_MAPPINGS[result] or {id = 1, name = "Informational"}

    -- Build optimized output structure
    local output = {
        -- OCSF Required Fields
        class_uid = 1006,
        class_name = "File System Activity",
        category_uid = 1,
        category_name = "System Activity",
        activity_id = activity.id,
        activity_name = activity.name,
        severity_id = severity.id,
        severity = severity.name,
        time = record.time or os.time() * 1000,
        
        -- Event Specific Fields
        session_id = session_id,
        actor = {
            user = {
                name = username
            }
        },
        event_type = event_type,
        status = result,
        message = message
    }

    -- Add optional file info if present
    if file_path and file_path ~= "" then
        output.file = {
            path = file_path
        }
    end

    -- Extract and add IP if present
    local ip = raw:match("remote_ip=\"([^\"]+)\"")
    if ip then
        output.src_endpoint = {
            ip = ip
        }
    end

    -- Extract numeric fields
    local file_size = raw:match("fileSize=(%d+)")
    local bytes_transferred = raw:match("bytesTransferred=(%d+)")
    
    if file_size then
        output.file = output.file or {}
        output.file.size = tonumber(file_size)
    end
    
    if bytes_transferred then
        output.traffic = {
            bytes = tonumber(bytes_transferred)
        }
    end

    return output
end

-- Error handling wrapper
function safe_transform(record)
    local success, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if not success then
        return nil, string.format("Transform error: %s", result)
    end
    return result
end
